title: Synonym — BENEVOLENT
description: Choose the word closest in meaning to BENEVOLENT.
explanation: Benevolent means well-meaning and kind.

title: Antonym — DILIGENT
description: Choose the word opposite in meaning to DILIGENT.
explanation: Diligent means hardworking. Its opposite is lazy.

title: Sentence Correction — Subject-Verb Agreement
description:
Choose the correct sentence:
A) The team are playing well today.
B) The team is playing well today.
C) The teams is playing well today.
D) The team were playing well today.
explanation: 'Team' is a collective noun (singular) and takes 'is'. Option B is correct.

title: Fill in the Blank — Preposition
description: She has been living in Mumbai ___ 2015.
explanation: 'Since' is used with a point in time. 'For' is used with a duration.

title: Idiom — Meaning
description: What does the idiom 'burn the midnight oil' mean?
options: To work late into the night
correct_answer: To work late into the night
explanation: 'Burn the midnight oil' means to study or work late into the night.

title: One Word Substitution — Person
description: A person who walks in their sleep is called:
explanation: Somnambulist = a person who sleepwalks. Insomniac = can't sleep. Egotist = self-centered. Altruist = selfless.

title: Reading Comprehension — Main Idea
description:
Read: 'Artificial intelligence is transforming healthcare by enabling faster diagnoses, personalized treatments, and predictive analytics. Hospitals using AI-powered tools report 30% improvement in early disease detection. However, concerns about data privacy and algorithmic bias remain significant challenges.'

What is the main idea?
options: AI improves healthcare but raises concerns
Data privacy is the only issue with AI
correct_answer: AI improves healthcare but raises concerns
explanation: The passage discusses both benefits (faster diagnoses, better detection) and challenges (privacy, bias) of AI in healthcare.

title: Error Spotting — Tense
description: Find the error: 'He has went to the market yesterday.'
explanation: 'Has went' is incorrect. Either 'has gone' (present perfect) or 'went' (past simple with 'yesterday'). Since 'yesterday' is used, simple past 'went' is correct.

title: Synonym — PRAGMATIC
description: Choose the word closest in meaning to PRAGMATIC.
explanation: Pragmatic means dealing with things in a practical, realistic way rather than theoretical.

title: Antonym — EPHEMERAL
description: Choose the word opposite in meaning to EPHEMERAL.
explanation: Ephemeral means lasting for a very short time. Its opposite is permanent.

title: Para Jumbles — Reorder
description:
Arrange in correct order:
P. He was born in a small village.
Q. Today he is one of the richest people in India.
R. He started his first business at age 20.
S. Despite many failures, he persevered.
explanation: Logical order: Born in village (P) → Started business (R) → Persevered through failures (S) → Now rich (Q).

title: Sentence Correction — Pronoun
description:
Choose the correct sentence:
A) Me and him went to the store.
B) He and I went to the store.
C) Him and I went to the store.
D) Me and he went to the store.
explanation: Subject pronouns (He, I) should be used as the subject of a sentence, not object pronouns (him, me).

title: Fill in the Blank — Vocabulary
description: The professor's lecture was so ___ that half the audience fell asleep.
explanation: Monotonous means dull and repetitive, which would cause people to fall asleep.

title: Idiom — Hit the Nail
description: What does 'hit the nail on the head' mean?
explanation: 'Hit the nail on the head' means to describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem.

title: Reading Comprehension — Inference
description:
Read: 'The company announced a 15% pay cut for all executives while maintaining hourly workers' wages. The CEO publicly took a 30% cut. Employee satisfaction surveys showed a 20% increase in trust scores.'

What can be inferred?
options: Trust scores always increase during cuts
explanation: The passage links leadership pay cuts with increased trust scores, suggesting sacrifice built trust.

title: One Word Substitution — Place
description: A place where dead bodies are kept for identification is called:
explanation: Mortuary = place for keeping dead bodies. Cemetery = burial ground. Sanctuary = safe place. Archive = records storage.

title: Error Spotting — Article
description: Find the error: 'She is a honest woman and everyone respects her.'
explanation: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound (the 'h' is silent), so 'an' should be used instead of 'a'.

title: Synonym — UBIQUITOUS
description: Choose the word closest in meaning to UBIQUITOUS.
explanation: Ubiquitous means present, appearing, or found everywhere. Omnipresent has the same meaning.

title: Antonym — OSTENTATIOUS
description: Choose the word opposite in meaning to OSTENTATIOUS.
explanation: Ostentatious means designed to impress or attract attention. Modest is its opposite.

title: Cloze Test — Passage
description:
Fill in the blanks: 'Education is the most powerful ___(1)___ which you can use to change the world. It gives people the ___(2)___ to think critically.'

(1) and (2) are:
explanation: This is a famous Nelson Mandela quote. 'Weapon' (metaphorical tool) and 'ability' (capacity to think) fit best contextually.

title: Sentence Improvement
description:
'No sooner did he reached the station when the train left.'

Choose the correct version:
options: No sooner did he reach the station than the train left.
No sooner he reached the station than the train left.
No sooner did he reached the station than the train left.
No sooner did he reach the station when the train left.
correct_answer: No sooner did he reach the station than the train left.
explanation: 'No sooner...than' is the correct construction. After 'did', use base form 'reach' (not 'reached').

title: Vocabulary — Context
description: The politician's speech was full of ___ promises that no one believed.
explanation: Hollow means empty or insincere — promises that lack substance and aren't believed.

title: Reading Comprehension — Tone
description:
Read: 'While social media connects billions globally, its impact on mental health is alarming. Studies link excessive usage to anxiety, depression, and reduced self-esteem, particularly among teenagers. Perhaps it is time we reconsider our digital habits.'

What is the tone of the passage?
explanation: Words like 'alarming', 'anxiety', 'depression', and 'reconsider' indicate a cautionary tone.

title: Active-Passive Voice
description: Convert to passive voice: 'The manager approved the proposal.'
options: The proposal was approved by the manager.
The proposal has approved the manager.
The manager was approved by the proposal.
The proposal is approved by the manager.
correct_answer: The proposal was approved by the manager.
explanation: Simple past active → was/were + past participle + by agent.

title: Direct-Indirect Speech
description: Convert: He said, 'I am going to the market.'
options: He said that he was going to the market.
He said that I am going to the market.
He said that he is going to the market.
He told that he was going to the market.
correct_answer: He said that he was going to the market.
explanation: Direct to indirect: 'I' → 'he', 'am going' → 'was going' (tense shifts back).

title: Idiom — Spill the Beans
description: What does 'spill the beans' mean?
explanation: 'Spill the beans' means to reveal secret information, especially unintentionally.

title: One Word Substitution — Act
description: The act of killing a king is called:
explanation: Regicide = killing a king. Homicide = killing a person. Suicide = killing oneself. Genocide = killing a large group.

title: Antonym — LOQUACIOUS
description: Choose the word opposite in meaning to LOQUACIOUS.
explanation: Loquacious means very talkative. Taciturn means reserved or uncommunicative — the opposite.

title: Fill in the Blank — Conjunction
description: ___ he is rich, he lives a simple life.
explanation: 'Although' introduces a contrast: being rich (expectation of lavish life) vs. living simply.

title: Error Spotting — Word Choice
description: Find the error: 'The sceneries of Kashmir are very beautiful.'
explanation: 'Scenery' is an uncountable noun and has no plural form. 'The scenery of Kashmir is very beautiful.'

title: Reading Comprehension — Detail
description:
Read: 'The Indian space program, ISRO, successfully launched its Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) in 2013 at a cost of just $74 million — less than the budget of the Hollywood movie Gravity. It became the first Asian nation to reach Mars orbit.'

What made ISRO's Mars mission remarkable?
options: Its very low cost and first-attempt success
It was the most expensive space mission
correct_answer: Its very low cost and first-attempt success
explanation: The passage emphasizes the low cost ($74M, less than a movie) and being first Asian nation to reach Mars.

title: Synonym — METICULOUS
description: Choose the word closest in meaning to METICULOUS.
explanation: Meticulous means showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.

title: Para Jumbles — Topic Sentence
description: Arrange: P. Thus global warming is a serious threat. Q. Carbon emissions have increased by 40% since 1990. R. This has led to rising temperatures worldwide. S. Governments must act urgently to reduce emissions.
explanation: Logical flow: Emissions increased (Q) → Temperatures rose (R) → Serious threat (P) → Governments must act (S).

title: Vocabulary — Analogy
description: VERBOSE : CONCISE :: OPAQUE : ?
explanation: Verbose (wordy) is opposite of concise (brief). Opaque (not transparent) is opposite of transparent.

title: Sentence Correction — Parallelism
description:
Choose the correct sentence:
A) She likes hiking, swimming, and to ride bikes.
B) She likes hiking, swimming, and riding bikes.
C) She likes to hike, swimming, and riding bikes.
D) She likes to hiking, to swimming, and riding.
explanation: Parallel structure requires all items in a list to have the same form: hiking, swimming, riding (all gerunds).

title: Fill in the Blank — Phrasal Verb
description: The meeting was ___ due to lack of quorum.
explanation: 'Called off' means cancelled. 'Called on' = visited. 'Called up' = telephoned. 'Called for' = demanded.

title: One Word — Government
description: Government by a single person with absolute power is called:
explanation: Autocracy = one person with absolute power. Democracy = by the people. Oligarchy = by a few. Plutocracy = by the wealthy.

title: Comprehension — Critical Analysis
description:
Read: 'Remote work has increased productivity by 13% according to Stanford research. However, 67% of remote workers report feeling disconnected from colleagues. Companies are now experimenting with hybrid models to balance efficiency with human connection.'

What is the author suggesting?
options: Hybrid work balances productivity and connection
correct_answer: Hybrid work balances productivity and connection
explanation: The passage presents benefits of remote work, drawbacks of isolation, and suggests hybrid models as the balanced solution.

title: Idiom — Barking Up Wrong Tree
description: What does 'barking up the wrong tree' mean?
explanation: 'Barking up the wrong tree' means pursuing a mistaken or misguided course of action.

title: Error Spotting — Comparative
description: Find the error: 'She is more taller than her sister.'
explanation: 'Taller' is already comparative. Adding 'more' is redundant. Correct: 'She is taller than her sister.'

title: Synonym — COPIOUS
description: Choose the word closest in meaning to COPIOUS.
explanation: Copious means in large quantities; abundant or plentiful.

title: Sentence Completion — Logic
description: Despite the heavy rain, the match was ___ because the ground had excellent drainage.
explanation: 'Despite' indicates contrast. Heavy rain would normally cancel a match, but good drainage prevented this.

title: Active-Passive — Complex
description: Convert to passive: 'They will have completed the project by December.'
options: The project will have been completed by them by December.
The project will be completed by December.
The project had been completed by them.
The project is being completed by them.
correct_answer: The project will have been completed by them by December.
explanation: Future perfect active → will have been + past participle (passive).

title: Vocabulary — Nuance
description: Choose the most appropriate word: 'The judge's decision was fair and ___.'
explanation: Impartial means treating all equally; unbiased. It pairs naturally with 'fair'.

title: Reading Comp — Author's Purpose
description:
Read: 'Every year, 8 million tonnes of plastic enters our oceans. By 2050, there could be more plastic than fish by weight. We don't need an alternative to plastic — we need an alternative to our throwaway culture.'

What is the author's purpose?
options: To persuade people to change their consumption habits
To explain how plastic is made
correct_answer: To persuade people to change their consumption habits
explanation: The author uses alarming statistics and explicitly calls for changing 'throwaway culture' — a persuasive purpose.

title: One Word — Impossible to Read
description: Handwriting that is impossible to read is called:
explanation: Illegible = impossible to read. Legible = easy to read. Eligible = qualified. Edible = can be eaten.

title: Direct-Indirect — Question
description: Convert: She asked me, 'Where do you live?'
options: She asked me where I lived.
She asked me where do I live.
She asked me where did I live.
She told me where I live.
correct_answer: She asked me where I lived.
explanation: In indirect speech, the question becomes a statement: question word + subject + verb (past tense).

title: Antonym — CANDID
description: Choose the word opposite in meaning to CANDID.
explanation: Candid means truthful and straightforward. Secretive (hiding information) is the opposite.
